title: FBI RS 4.10.26

communications.communication:
Dear FOIA Officer:

You are receiving this correspondence as a request under the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. subsection 552. I am requesting the following:

Copies of all Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) records, documents, and communications responsive to Colonel Raymond Starrett Sleeper, aka Raymond Starrat Sleeper, aka Raymond Sterritt Sleeper, aka Raymond S. Sleeper (September 13-1914-April 14, 1995; SSN 001-14-5228).

Col. Sleeper served as the Commanding Officer of the United States Air Force's Foreign Technology Division (FTD), the Deputy Chief of the Strategic Vulnerability Branch in its Air Intelligence Division, and faculty at the Air War College. Additionally, he maintained personal affiliations with the Institute for American Strategy, later known as the American Security Council Foundation, and the Western Goals Foundation.

Col. Sleeper wrote on the subject of Soviet psychological warfare, publishing books like "A Lexicon of Marxist-Leninist Semantics" and "Mesmerized by the Bear: The Soviet of Strategy of Deception".

Proof of death is not required as Col. Sleeper's date of birth was over 100 years ago; however, his gravestone is listed on a public database, Find a Grave, and can be found here: https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/149575947/raymond-starrett-sleeper

I am asking you waive any fees associated with this request. I am a member of the news media. My bylines, which include Unicorn Riot, Covert Action Magazine, and the Geopolitical Economy Report, can be found in both print and electronic publications, and I am sending this request in coordination with Muckrock, an award-winning and nationally-recognized news organization. Please take note of the Office of Management and Budget guidelines published March 27, 1987 (52 FR 10012) that include electronic publications and other nontraditional publishers as representatives of the news media.

A fee waiver is appropriate in situations where "disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government, and is not primarily in the commercial interest of the requester,” 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(iii). While with the FTD, Col. Sleeper was briefly in charge of overseeing the United States Air Force's Project Blue Book, a study into unidentified flying objects (UFOs). Historical disclosures by the United States Air Force and Central Intelligence Agency indicate programs like Project Blue Book were used to deceive the American public about the existence (or non-existence) of UFOs. A 1994 report issued by the Air Force, "The Roswell Report: Case Closed", states that a 1947 UFO crash near Roswell, New Mexico, was in fact a "compartmented, sensitive effort" involving balloon reconnaissance research named Project MOGUL, according to pages 24 through 29. A copy can be found here:  https://www.dafhistory.af.mil/Portals/16/documents/AFD-101201-038.pdf

Germane to Project Blue Book, a 1997 article in the Central Intelligence Agency's "Studies in Intelligence" journal titled "CIA's Role in the Study of UFO's, 1947-1990" observes collaboration between agency officials and Blue Book investigators. Gerald Haines, the article's author, writes on page 73: "The early U-2s were silver (they were later painted black) and reflected the rays from the sun, especially at sunrise and sunset. They often appeared as fiery objects to observers below. Air Force BLUE BOOK investigators aware of the secret U-2 flights tried to explain away such sightings by linking them to natural phenomena such as ice crystals and temperature inversions. By checking with the Agency's U-2 Project Staff in Washington, BLUE BOOK investigators were able to attribute many UFO sightings to U-2 flights. They were careful, however, not to reveal the true cause of the sighting to the public." A copy of the article can be found here: https://www.cia.gov/resources/csi/static/cia-role-study-UFOs.pdf

Attached is correspondence, in the form of a letter taken from Project Blue Book's archives, verifying Col. Sleeper's connection to the program. As Col. Sleeper was involved with Project Blue Book and both the Air Force and CIA have acknowledged this program and others like it were used to deceive the American public, any records generated by the FBI about Col. Sleeper would contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government as it relates to its research into UFOs and other relevant subject areas. The letter can also be found here: https://www.fold3.com/image/7282652/tucson-arizona-blank-page-8-us-project-blue-book-ufo-investigations-1947-1969

This request is not being made for commercial purposes. All documents will be provided to the general public without charge.

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Records System, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for all known identities for Col. Sleeper in both main-file records and cross-reference records related to the Human Resources Research Institute, Institute for American Strategy, American Security Council Foundation, and Western Goals Foundation.

In the event that there are fees, I am willing to pay up to $100. Please advise if fees will exceed this amount so I might make an arrangement for requesting the records. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

I look forward to your response within 20 working days, as the statute requires. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

Sincerely,

Robert Skvarla, Jr.

title: Obama Presidential Library: Targeted Search: Gali District Disenfranchisement (2014)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: Targeted Search: Gali District Disenfranchisement (2014)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request all records, including emails (sent, received, CC’d, or BCC’d) and their attachments, created between April 1, 2014, and January 20, 2015, that discuss the disenfranchisement and "passportization" of ethnic Georgians in the Gali district of Abkhazia.

To ensure a comprehensive search of the National Security Council (NSC) and Department of State records, please include the following individuals and their associated email handles/aliases in your query:

1. Primary Department of State Targets:

Tom Malinowski (Assistant Secretary, DRL): malinowskit@state.gov, malinowskitp@state.gov, and any alias associated with "A/S Malinowski."
Richard Norland (Ambassador to Georgia): norlandr@state.gov, norlandrc@state.gov, and any alias associated with "Ambassador Norland" or "COM Tbilisi."
Eric Rubin (Deputy Assistant Secretary, EUR): rubine@state.gov.

2. National Security Council (NSC) Targets:

Celeste Wallander (Senior Director for Russia and Central Asia): cwallander@nsc.eop.gov.
Charles Kupchan (Senior Director for European Affairs): ckupchan@nsc.eop.gov.
Elizabeth Sherwood-Randall (White House Coordinator for Defense Policy and Arms Control): esherwood-randall@nsc.eop.gov.

NSC Records Management: Search for all records within the "Europe and Eurasia Directorate" and the "International Organizations and Alliance Directorate."

3. Specific Subject Matter Keywords & Metadata:
Please perform a keyword search against the "Subject" and "Body" fields of the above accounts for:

Gali AND Passport* (to include passportization/passports)
Ankvab AND Khajimba
Abkhaz* AND Disenfranchis*
Surkov AND Abkhaz*
Gali Georgians
IDP status
Аԥсны Аҳәынҭқарра 
Республика Абхазия

4. Search Constraints:
I request that the search include both the Open (Unclassified) Side and the Classified Side (SIPRNet/JWICS) records for the requested period. If any records are withheld under a (b)(1) national security exemption, I request a Vaughn Index describing the withheld material and the justification for the exemption.

Thank you to the Obama Presidential Library for the library's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

title: NIH Grant No. 1R37CA300386

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: NIH Grant No. 1R37CA300386
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Project Number: 1R37CA300386
Project Title: A Single, Universal, and Digitally Programmable RF System Enables MRI of Any Nucleus
Principal Investigator: HAN, VICTOR 

Program Official: ZHANG, HUIMING
Opportunity Number: PA-25-301
Organization: UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA BERKELEY
NIH Institute or Center: National Cancer Institute
Project Start Date: 01-April-2026

Specifically, I request the following records, to the extent they are available and releasable:
1. The funded grant application in its final submitted form.
2. The abstract, specific aims, research strategy, budget pages, and other components included in the awarded application.
3. Any revised or resubmitted funded version of the application, if applicable.
4. All funded progress reports for the award, including each annual Research Performance Progress Report (RPPR), any multi-year funded progress reports if applicable, and the final RPPR or other closeout progress report, to the extent maintained by NIH and releasable under FOIA.
5. Any other grant application materials maintained by NIH that are part of the awarded application file and are subject to release under FOIA.

This request is limited to existing agency records. I am not requesting the creation of new records, explanations, analyses, or responses to questions.
If any portion of the requested material is exempt from disclosure, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions. For each withheld portion, please identify the basis for withholding, including the applicable FOIA exemption(s).


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Sterman

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2261817/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

title: 2024 to Present AI Usage Records

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: 2024 to Present AI Usage Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All generative AI conversation logs — including user prompts/inputs and AI-generated outputs/responses — created by city employees using any of the following platforms: Microsoft Copilot ($45K budgeted FY 2024-2025), ChatGPT Enterprise, and any other generative AI chatbot tool. Time period: January 1, 2024 to present.

2. Administrative usage logs or reports showing the number of employees with access to, and usage statistics for, each generative AI platform deployed by the city during this period.

3. For ChatGPT Enterprise accounts: the data export available through the admin console (ZIP archive containing conversation.html files and metadata JSON). For Microsoft 365 Copilot: the conversation history logs available through the M365 admin center or Purview compliance portal.

This request encompasses all city employees and contractors with access to these tools. I am not requesting the tools' training data or source code — only the conversation logs between users and AI systems.

If any records are withheld, please cite the specific statutory exemption and release all segregable portions.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Raghav Sinha

title: FOIA: Standard Records Request - Frederick, MD

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: FOIA: Standard Records Request - Frederick, MD
communications.communication:
April 8, 2026

Re: Public Records Request — Holiday Lighting Procurement Records

Dear Records Custodian,

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act (PIA) (Md. Code, GP § 4-101), I am writing to request access to and copies of the following public records from Frederick:

all records related to holiday lighting and seasonal decorative lighting services, including but not limited to: contracts,   ▎  purchase orders, invoices, bid submissions, vendor proposals, requests for proposals (RFPs), requests for qualifications     ▎ (RFQs), scope of work documents, vendor selection criteria, evaluation scoring sheets, and any correspondence with vendors    ▎ or contractors related to holiday or seasonal lighting services.

This request covers 10 years.

As you may be aware, the Maryland Public Information Act (PIA) requires a response within 10 days of receipt of this request.

I would prefer to receive these records in electronic format (PDF, Excel, or other digital format) delivered via email to rfp@britenites.com. If electronic delivery is not available, please advise on alternative arrangements.

Regarding fees: I request a fee waiver as this information is being sought in the public interest. If a fee waiver is not available, please provide a cost estimate before proceeding with the request if anticipated fees will exceed $25.00.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption and provide any non-exempt portions that can be reasonably segregated.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me at rfp@britenites.com if you have any questions or need clarification.

Sincerely,
Dillon Williams
rfp@britenites.com

Good morning, Mr. Williams.

In your April 8, 2026 message to the Baltimore City Department of Public Works (“DPW”), you requested records related to holiday lighting and seasonal decorative lighting services by the city.  The Maryland Public Information Act (“PIA”), codified in §§ 4-101 through 4-601 of the General Provisions Article (“GP”) of the Maryland Code governs your request.

This response is on behalf of DPW.  After review, DPW has determined that it has no records that are responsive to your request. Each Baltimore City agency is the custodian of its own records. See Baltimore City Code, Article 1 § 10-1. If responsive records exist, they may be in the custody and control of the Department of Finance’s Bureau of Procurement. For a complete list of agency custodians, please see https://oag.maryland.gov/resources-info/Documents/pdfs/Appendix_J.pdf

Nothing in this response is intended to indicate that any records sought from City agencies exist or to waive any privileges held by the Mayor and City Council.  You may contest this response by filing a complaint in Circuit Court pursuant to GP § 4-362.

communications.subject: Records Request Confirmation
communications.communication:
Dear Lia Jones, 
  
 Thank you for submitting a request for public information. This is your Request Number: 2026-102-MPIA and your security key is EB1B765A. Please retain this information, as it will be required when communicating with the City Clerk regarding your request. 
  
 Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, the City will provide an initial response within 10 working days of receipt of your request. If additional time is required to complete the request, you will be notified accordingly. If you did not provide contact information at the time of submission, you are responsible for contacting the City Clerk’s Office to confirm receipt and check the status of your request. In all communications or when picking up records, please reference your request number and security key. 
  
 Md. Code, Gen. Prov. Art., § 4-206 permits the City to charge reasonable fees for the search for and response to a Public Information Act request. The City charges a fee equivalent to the employee working on the request’s hourly pay times the number of hours worked. The first two hours are free. 
  
 If you have any questions or need assistance, please contact: Lia Jones, City Clerk, Phone: 240-805-1084, Email: cityclerk@gaithersburgmd.gov.  You may also use this system to upload any additional documents related to your request.

communications.subject: PIA - Request Submitted - PIA0003075
communications.communication:
April 10, 2026 
Dillon Williams 
208515-26725061@requests.muckrock.com 

Hello, 
This following is to confirm receipt of your request to Howard County Government’s Office of County Administration, requesting information in accordance with the Maryland Public Information Act, 4-101 et seq. of the General Provisions Article of the Annotated Code of Maryland (the “PIA”), which this office received 04-10-2026. 
Specifically, you asked for "Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act (PIA) (Md. Code, GP § 4-101), I am writing to request access to and copies of the following public records from Ellicott City: 
all records related to holiday lighting and seasonal decorative lighting services, including but not limited to: contracts, ▎ purchase orders, invoices, bid submissions, vendor proposals, requests for proposals (RFPs), requests for qualifications ▎ (RFQs), scope of work documents, vendor selection criteria, evaluation scoring sheets, and any correspondence with vendors ▎ or contractors related to holiday or seasonal lighting services. 
This request covers 10 years. 
Upon receipt of your request, a custodian must grant or deny the request not more than 30 days after receiving the request. However, if a custodian believes it will take more than 10 business days to respond to the request, the custodian must notify the requestor in writing or by email within that time period of the following: the amount of time it will take to produce the public record, an estimate of fees (if applicable) and the reason for the delay. Additionally, please note that under Section 4-206 of the PIA, a custodian may charge a fee for the search for, preparation of, and reproduction of a public record; this fee prorated for each individual's salary and actual time attributable to the search for and preparation of a public record. In addition, per Howard County Council Resolution No. 68-2022, the County charges a copying fee of $0.25 per page for a black and white paper copy. If electronic copies of the responsive records exist, they are free of charge; however, when an electronic response, or portion thereof, is too large to send electronically, the response, or portion thereof, will be provided either in paper format, or electronically on a CD, DVD or flash drive, as the requester specifies. The cost of a CD, DVD or flash drive is the actual cost of the CD, DVD, or flash drive. 
For more information on the PIA, please visit Maryland’s Office of the Attorney General website at http://www.marylandattorneygeneral.gov/Pages/OpenGov/pia.aspx. (http://www.marylandattorneygeneral.gov/Pages/OpenGov/pia.aspx) 
Sincerely, 
Keyonna Kinsler

title: CPRA - Ventura County - Flock Safety Documents (Ventura County Sheriff's Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA - Ventura County - Flock Safety Documents (Ventura County Sheriff's Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Organization Audit in Flock. 
All data logged from July 1, 2023 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, this report is available within the Insights tab and reflects searches conducted within the agency.

- Network Audit in Flock. 
All data logged from July 1, 2023 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, this report is available within the Insights tab and reflects searches of the agency's Flock network by any agency in the Flock system.

- Hardware.
The current number and installed locations of any Flock Safety, LPR,  AVIS, or machine learning-assisted camera systems (provide locations as coordinates or address with nearest cross-street), and any documents related to planned future installations of the same.

- Documents.
>  Invoices, contracts, purchase orders, and bid documents related to the acquisition or renewal of Flock Safety products or services
>  Agreements with homeowner associations, private businesses, or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems
>  Training materials, policies, and SOPs related to Flock Safety platform use
>  Grant or funding records related to the Flock system
>  Data retention policies for data collected by Flock Safety systems
>  Any complaints or grievances received from the public regarding ALPR camera placement or data use


I am not requesting personally identifiable information or license plate data. If any responsive record contains such information, please redact only that portion and produce the remainder. If any records are withheld, please provide a written index identifying each withheld record and the statutory exemption claimed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jordan
